column1: # comment: "First public crosswalk between the UK Information Exchange Standard (IES) and the Higher Quality Data Model (HQDM). Backbone (4D) correspondences only in v0.1. All correspondences are candidate-for-review, hand-curated from the published ontologies (IES-Org/ont-ies ies-common.ttl; gchq/HQDM hqdm.owl) and grounded in the shared BORO / ISO 15926 4D tradition. Confidence is the curator's subjective strength. See DIVERGENCES.md for pairs that do NOT cleanly map."

column8: Both are the abstract root of the ontology. IES Thing also parents abstract (non-4D) classes; HQDM thing parents abstract_object and spatio_temporal_extent.
IES Element is "a Thing that has a spatial extent and can have start and end dates", the 4D individual root. Direct counterpart of the HQDM 4D root.

IES Entity = a whole-life, persisting thing (Person, Device, Organisation...). HQDM individual = a whole-life spatio_temporal_extent. Close; HQDM physical_object is a narrower alternative for tangible entities.
Both model a temporal part of an individual. Structural divergence: IES State is a top-level root class; HQDM state is subsumed under spatio_temporal_extent. See DIVERGENCES.md #3.
IES Event is "an activity or incident, involving one or more participants", a happening. Its true HQDM counterpart is activity, NOT hqdm:event. See DIVERGENCES.md #1.
FALSE FRIEND. hqdm:event is an instantaneous temporal boundary (a point), not a happening. Recorded only to warn against the name-based mapping. See DIVERGENCES.md #1.
IES bounds temporal extents with BoundingState (a State at the start/end); HQDM bounds them with event (a point) via beginning/ending. Same job, different ontological device. See DIVERGENCES.md #2.
Both are a temporal extent that is everywhere in space and bounded in time.
IES ParticularPeriod is an ISO8601-anchored particular; maps to a HQDM period_of_time individual (an instance), not the class.

Both name a maximal consistent 4D whole. IES PossibleWorld is subsumed under Element (it is itself a spatio-temporal extent); HQDM possible_world is likewise a spatio_temporal_extent. See DIVERGENCES.md #5.

Strong convergence: both treat participation as a State / temporal part of the participating thing, not as a relation only. IES EventParticipant is a subclass of State; HQDM participant is a state_of_X.
Both open the powertype hierarchy over 4D individuals. IES adds a second-order ClassOfClassOfElement mirrored by HQDM class_of_class_of_spatio_temporal_extent.
Mereological part-of over spatio-temporal extents on both sides.
Relates a participation-state to the activity/event it is part of.
A State isStateOf its Entity ~ a state is a temporal_part_of its individual. Close but not identical: isStateOf is typed to State->Entity, temporal_part_of is general.
IES isStartOf links a BoundingState to what it starts; HQDM beginning links a spatio_temporal_extent to its start event. Related via the boundary-device divergence (#2).
